preamble: The shift from the voiced aspirated alveolar stop [dʱ] to its palatalized counterpart [dʲ] represents secondary palatalization, a phonetic process where consonants acquire palatal features, often triggered by adjacent front vowels or as part of systematic consonant inventory reorganization.

examples.note: Palatalization of post-alveolar/aspirated d in Slavic languages is widespread and well-documented in synchronic Russian phonology.
